draft.title: a minimalist journal app
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a minimalist journal app

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: A minimalist journal app benefits from rounded, gentle edges rather than sharp geometry
facade.label: Soft corners
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward approachable, tactile feel; likely favors friendly typography and cushioned spacing; may want warmth in neutrality
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers crisp precision; drawn to grid-based layouts and defined edges; minimalism = austere, not cozy

evidence.hypothesis: A minimalist journal app benefits from rounded, gentle edges rather than sharp geometry
evidence.implication: User gravitates toward approachable, tactile feel; likely favors friendly typography and cushioned spacing; may want warmth in neutrality

facade.hypothesis: Users of minimalist journal apps prefer gentle, forgiving interfaces over sharp, precise interactions
facade.label: Soft blur transitions
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use gradual fades, blurred overlays, and smooth opacity changes—creating a calming, dreamlike quality that feels intimate and unhurried.
facade.rejectImplies: The user favors crisp, immediate visual feedback—sharp edges, instant state changes, and clear delineation between interface elements; a cool, snappy, digitally precise aesthetic.

facade.hypothesis: Fixed sidebar navigation reduces cognitive load for journaling by keeping entry categories constantly accessible, without requiring menu toggles.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation persistence: persistent sidebar vs. collapsible/hamburger menu
facade.label: Sidebar always visible
facade.acceptImplies: User values stable, always-present navigation anchors; likely prefers predictable, low-interaction patterns; may expect a traditional app layout.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers minimal chrome and maximum writing space; likely values distraction-free interfaces; may favor hidden navigation that reveals on demand.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gradual animated reveals over instant static appearance when adding journal entries
facade.axisTargeted: Animation presence and timing in entry interactions
facade.label: Entries fade in
facade.acceptImplies: Embracing motion-based feedback for data presentation; entries should animate into view; transitions signal app responsiveness
facade.rejectImplies: Static, instant-load preference; entries appear at full opacity immediately; motion may feel unnecessary or distracting in a minimal interface

facade.hypothesis: Users expect chronological entries as stackable cards rather than a continuous scrolling feed
facade.axisTargeted: entry presentation format — cards vs continuous text stream
facade.label: Daily log as cards
facade.acceptImplies: Favor discrete, tappable entry units with clear visual boundaries and date headers; consider card-based navigation and flip/expand interactions
facade.rejectImplies: Favor a continuous scrolling timeline or narrative flow; entries blend together without hard visual separation; less need for individual entry 'objects'

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal visual design (clean, sparse) vs. design with subtle visual richness (textures, depth, color)
Whether user wants linear, chronological entry flow vs. flexible, non-linear organization (tags, collections, search-first)
Whether user wants neutral, invisible interface vs. warm, encouraging tone that acknowledges their writing
Whether user prefers bright, energizing environment vs. calm, introspective (dark/muted) space for reflection
Whether user wants entries to be simple text-only vs. structured with metadata (dates, moods, categories visible)
Whether user prefers instant, snappy interactions vs. gentle, deliberate transitions that slow them down

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal visual design (clean, sparse) vs. design with subtle visual richness (textures, depth, color)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants linear, chronological entry flow vs. flexible, non-linear organization (tags, collections, search-first)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants neutral, invisible interface vs. warm, encouraging tone that acknowledges their writing
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers bright, energizing environment vs. calm, introspective (dark/muted) space for reflection
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants entries to be simple text-only vs. structured with metadata (dates, moods, categories visible)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers instant, snappy interactions vs. gentle, deliberate transitions that slow them down
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A minimalist journal app with serif fonts conveys thoughtfulness and permanence, contrasting with the roundness already accepted
facade.label: Serifed typography
facade.acceptImplies: User values classical, intentional feeling; may want entries to feel substantial and literary rather than purely modern
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean, contemporary sans-serif aesthetic; wants approachability over formality; likely leans minimal and light

facade.hypothesis: Users want a minimalist journal app with a serene, understated voice that feels like a personal refuge rather than a productivity tool
facade.label: Quiet and calm
facade.acceptImplies: Voice should be gentle, spacious, meditative. Possibly warm but never cheerful. Copy emphasizes reflection over achievement.
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer a more upbeat, encouraging tone. Or they want neutral/functional language. Serene tone may feel too precious or slow for this use case.

draft.title: Daily Reflection
draft.summary: A minimalist journal app for capturing daily thoughts with a clean, distraction-free interface. Focus on simplicity and ease of entry.
draft.html:
Today

Monday, January 13
